chapter_title.ar: باب دُخُولِ الْقَبْرِ وَ الْخُرُوجِ مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 62 – Entering the grave and the exiting from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف مرفوع مضم ر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/87)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف مرفوع مضم ر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/87)
text: قَالَ يَدْخُلُ الرَّجُلُ الْقَبْرَ مِنْ حَيْثُ شَاءَ وَ لَا يَخْرُجْ إِلَّا مِنْ قِبَلِ رِجْلَيْهِ .
وَ فِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِنَّ لِكُلِّ بَيْتٍ بَاباً وَ إِنَّ بَابَ الْقَبْرِ مِنْ قِبَلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, raising it, said,
‘He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The man can enter the grave from wherever he so desires to, and he cannot exit except from the side of his legs’.
And in another report, he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘For every house there is a door, and the door of the grave is from the side of the two legs’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad in a marfu‘ manner the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘One may enter a grave from whatever direction he likes, but should come out from the side where the feet are placed.’”
